figures.caption: FIG. 4: Percentage fractional error (i.e. ϵ× 100) calculated by averaging over 40 trajectories of 500 steps at energy 1/8 for 81 parameter pairs in [0.1, 0.9]2 (the white crosses indicate parameter points in the training set). This is calculated using trajectories generated by ASRNNs for three cases, one without noise and the others with significant noise and different extents of correlation, note the difference on the colorbar limits in the different cases.

FIG. 6: Estimated parameters obtained from symbolic regression with PySINDy for data generated by trained ASRNNs (with various extents of noise) with the Henon Heiles potential. Panel (a) indicates the results from a seen parameter set (0.4, 0.6) and panel (b) considers an unseen set (0.5, 0.7). Data is generated by an ensemble of 40 models and regressed independently, the estimated parameters are then averaged over this ensemble, errorbars indicate 1σ deviations.

FIG. 7: Learned potential energy functions for the Morse system under different extents of noise averaged over an ensemble of 40 models, the regions indicate 1σ deviation and dashed line indicates the true potential curve. Here α = 0.5 is a seen parameter while the others are unseen.

FIG. 10: Learned (solid line) and true (dashed line) potential profiles for the double well potential for a sample of 10 models. For each model the left panel considers unseen values of α in the training region (single minimum), right panel displays the bifurcation point α = 0 and other α < 0 (two minima).
FIG. 1: Recurrent unit of an ASRNN, here we take (qn,pn;λ) as input and implement the Verlet algorithm which returns (qn+1,pn+1;λ) where the n indexes time steps separated by the chosen time step ∆t.
